544.reason: The line counter is not incremented when a newline character is encountered inside a block comment, which will cause incorrect line numbers for all subsequent tokens.

556.reason: [Handles lexical error type 'a' for single '&'. Returning INNER_ERROR immediately may disrupt the token stream processing loop in pass() and lacks proper character consumption for error recovery.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Handles lexical error type 'a' for single '&'. Returning INNER_ERROR immediately may disrupt the token stream processing loop in pass() and lacks proper character consumption for error recovery.]

565.reason: [Handles lexical error type 'a' for single '|'. Same issue as above: returning INNER_ERROR halts normal tokenization flow and does not safely skip the invalid symbol for continued parsing.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Handles lexical error type 'a' for single '|'. Same issue as above: returning INNER_ERROR halts normal tokenization flow and does not safely skip the invalid symbol for continued parsing.]

979.reason: [should call 'next()' instead of 'lexer.next()' because 'lexer' is not defined in this scope; this will cause a compilation error since 'lexer' variable doesn't exist locally]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [should call 'next()' instead of 'lexer.next()' because 'lexer' is not defined in this scope; this will cause a compilation error since 'lexer' variable doesn't exist locally]

496.reason: [When a single '&' is encountered, the code correctly detects the error but fails to consume the character properly. The current 'ch' (which is the character after '&') remains unprocessed, potentially causing issues in subsequent token recognition. More critically, the token string at this point contains "&" + next_char, but we should only have processed the single '&' before reporting the error.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [When a single '&' is encountered, the code correctly detects the error but fails to consume the character properly. The current 'ch' (which is the character after '&') remains unprocessed, potentially causing issues in subsequent token recognition. More critically, the token string at this point contains "&" + next_char, but we should only have processed the single '&' before reporting the error.]

505.reason: [Same issue as with '&': when a single '|' is encountered, the lexer reads the next character into 'ch' and adds it to the token, but then reports an error without properly handling the token state. The single '|' should be recognized as the erroneous token, not "|" + following character.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue as with '&': when a single '|' is encountered, the lexer reads the next character into 'ch' and adds it to the token, but then reports an error without properly handling the token state. The single '|' should be recognized as the erroneous token, not "|" + following character.]
